product_overview: Regkasse Admin Panel is a Next.js-based administration interface for the Regkasse POS platform, supporting authenticated access, backend health checks, multi-tenant operations, and user/role management. It uses token-based authentication and API-driven UI flows to manage platform and tenant administration tasks.
core_goals: Provide secure login, token refresh, logout, and current-user profile retrieval for admin/POS users.
Expose a lightweight health check endpoint for operational monitoring and test readiness.
Support correct user-role assignment flows with full role catalog visibility, proper selection state, and backend validation error handling.
Maintain reliable API-driven state with query invalidation and loading/error handling for admin workflows.
Enable tenant-aware and multi-tenant operations without cross-tenant data leakage.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Allows admin and POS users to sign in with email or username, retrieve the current session user, refresh access tokens, and end one or all sessions.
user_flows: POST /api/Auth/login with valid email-or-username and password -> Receive 200 with access token/session data -> GET /api/Auth/me with bearer token -> Receive 200 with authenticated user profile
POST /api/Auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 or 400 authentication error -> Display login failure message to the user
POST /api/Auth/refresh with a valid refresh token -> Receive 200 with new access token -> GET /api/Auth/me with new bearer token -> Receive 200 with current user profile
GET /api/Auth/me without an access token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> Prompt user to log in again
POST /api/Auth/logout with bearer token -> Receive 200 or success response -> Subsequent GET /api/Auth/me returns 401 for the ended session
POST /api/Auth/logout-all with bearer token -> Receive 200 or success response -> All active sessions for the user are invalidated -> GET /api/Auth/me with old token returns 401

features.name: API Health Monitoring
features.description: Provides a basic unauthenticated liveness/readiness endpoint to confirm the backend is reachable and operational.
user_flows: GET /api/health without authentication -> Receive 200 with healthy status payload -> Confirm backend is reachable before integration tests
GET /api/health during backend downtime -> Receive 5xx or network error -> Surface service unavailable state to monitoring tools
GET /api/health on a misconfigured environment or broken deployment -> Receive non-200 response -> Mark backend as unhealthy and block dependent checks

features.name: User Role Assignment
features.description: Displays the full role catalog when creating or editing a user, while showing only the selected user’s assigned role as checked; supports save, refresh, and backend validation error mapping.
user_flows: GET /api/UserManagement/roles when opening the user create or edit drawer -> Receive 200 with the full role catalog -> GET /api/UserManagement/{id} for an existing user -> Receive 200 with the user’s current single role -> Display all roles with only the assigned role selected -> PUT /api/UserManagement/{id} with a valid role -> Receive 200 and persist the updated role

GET /api/UserManagement/roles when the edit drawer opens slowly or on first load -> Receive 200 after loading -> Show a loading state until roles are available -> GET /api/UserManagement/{id} -> Receive 200 and sync the form to the current user

GET /api/UserManagement/{id} with a selected user that has a role not present in the initial fallback list -> Receive 200 with the user role -> Refresh role catalog via GET /api/UserManagement/roles -> Receive 200 with the complete catalog -> Render the full list and keep only the assigned role checked

PUT /api/UserManagement/{id} with an empty required field such as Unit or invalid role data -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation errors -> Map backend field errors onto the corresponding form controls -> Keep the modal open and show inline error messages

PUT /api/UserManagement/{id} after changing a user role -> Receive 200 -> Invalidate GET /api/UserManagement/{id} and list queries -> Re-open the same user and confirm the updated role is loaded from the backend

GET /api/UserManagement/roles or GET /api/UserManagement/{id} returns 404 or another error -> Show an error state in the drawer or form -> Prevent saving until data is available and the request succeeds

routes.description: Logout all sessions for current user

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Login and token-based auth flows for admin/POS users
user_interactions: Send email or username with password to login endpoint
Use returned bearer token for authorized endpoints
Refresh access token with refresh token

features.name: API Health Monitoring
user_interactions: Call health endpoint before running integration tests

known_limitations.issue: TestSprite MCP config may default to frontend mode and require manual correction for backend-only suites.
known_limitations.impact: Backend plan generation can fail until config is aligned.
